page: Amazon Web Services u7i-6tb.112xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 448 vCPUs across 224 physical cores using the x86_64 Intel Xeon 8477C processor running at 1.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 6144.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 13.71 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores placing in the top 10 percent. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, reaching 29269.53 GB/sec for cached reads and 840.11 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average and strong tiers respectively. The server demonstrates exceptional throughput in database-like operations, achieving 375.58 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers high memory capacity and massive parallel processing capabilities, making it highly efficient for consolidated enterprise workloads. However, there are notable tradeoffs. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, and the absence of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning tasks. While prompt processing for a 7B LLM model is strong at 199.93 tokens per second, text generation speeds are average to weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated accelerators.

This instance type is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise-grade SAP HANA deployments, massive data warehousing, and heavy parallel computing tasks that require substantial memory footprints alongside high vCPU counts.
description: Amazon Web Services u7i-6tb.112xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 448 vCPUs and 224 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8477C processors on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 6144.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 13.71 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside exceptional Redis throughput of 375.58 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is moderate, and LLM text generation is relatively weak due to the lack of dedicated GPUs. This server is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, making it an appropriate fit for large-scale in-memory databases, data warehousing, and massive parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u7i-6tb.112xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 448 vCPUs, 6144 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network speed, designed for massive in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services u7i-6tb.112xlarge features 448 vCPUs, 6 TB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for large-scale in-memory databases.
tagline: A massive memory-optimized platform featuring six terabytes of DDR5 RAM and four hundred forty-eight vCPUs for enterprise in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: 448 vCPUs and 224 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8477C processors
6144.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise data warehousing
